change_summary: Cycle-4 verification of three c3-derived patches. ISS-1872 (major): code_analysis.monitoring flipped hardcoded→none. ISS-1873 (moderate): detail_verdict_text second paragraph rewritten from generic Part-3.1b smoking-gun framing to WIN-018-specific no-instrumentation framing. ISS-1874 (minor): detail_evidence advocate-preempt sentence inserted clarifying that the WIN label reads simply RMS without obliquity qualifier, making the cherry-picking accusation about disclosure failure rather than mere component selection. All three patches verified present in origin/main HEAD; rendered docs/index.html computed counts (42/69 none, 25 hardcoded, 2 live) match data/wins.json post-patch state.
our_argument_summary: WIN-018 (Analemma Day Length RMS CONFIRMED 6.9 min) remains tagged Misleading. The 6.9-min figure is the RMS of the obliquity sub-component of the equation of time only (A/sqrt(2) where A=9.87 → 6.98 min); the full equation of time RMS is ~8.8 min and the swing is +16.4/−14.3 min (range 30.7). The dome label reads simply RMS, implying the headline figure, while quietly substituting a sub-statistic — the disclosure failure is what makes the choice cherry-picking rather than transparent precision. Standard physics derives all of this from Earth's 23.45° axial tilt and 0.0167 orbital eccentricity; the dome borrows the tilt directly (see WIN-056 Self-Contradicted) without dome-geometry derivation. Live monitor.py inspection (2026-05-07, 39+ numbered domains) confirms WIN-018 has no domain entry — no fetch, no comparison, no hardcoded predicted/observed pair. The c3 patches sharpened all three weak points without changing the verdict.

relevant_files: raw-text/02-wins.txt — single line: "WIN-018 — Solar Analemma Day Length RMS CONFIRMED (6.9 min)"

monitor.py (live, fetched 2026-05-07 from raw.githubusercontent.com/John09289/predictions/main/monitor.py) — 41 domains.append() blocks; grep for analemma|equation_of_time|equation.of.time|obliquity|6\.9|day.length returns zero matches; grep for WIN-018 returns zero matches in source attribution strings (which do reference WIN-001/008/012/015/024/026/039/046/047/056/061/065/073/075/076 explicitly)
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: None for WIN-018. The dome monitor pipeline runs append-style domain registration; WIN-018 receives no append() call. The value 6.9 lives only as static prose in 02-wins.txt and on the rendered WINs page.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Neither — and that is the whole point. The dome's static-win pattern (e.g. domains attributed to WIN-008, WIN-024, WIN-056, WIN-073, WIN-075, WIN-076) bake both predicted and observed into the same domain block — that is the bar "hardcoded" is supposed to clear. WIN-018 does not even reach that bar. Decorative claim, never instrumented.
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: None — there is nothing to evaluate.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None as of c4. The c3-flagged tag inconsistency (monitoring=hardcoded vs evidence-text "no domain in monitor.py") was closed by ISS-1872. Tag now correctly reads "none".

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Verified via live fetch of raw.githubusercontent.com/John09289/predictions/main/monitor.py at 2026-05-07T~14Z. The script defines its domains via domains.append({...}) calls (41 such blocks at HEAD; c3 baseline reported 39, suggesting 1-2 dome-side additions in the past ~12h, none related to analemma). Grep for analemma|equation_of_time|equation.of.time|obliquity|6\.9|day.length over the entire 1069-line file returns zero matches. WIN-018 has no live fetch, no hardcoded predicted/observed pair, no domain entry of any kind. "none" is the only correct tag.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: The equation of time and its decomposition into obliquity (~9.87 min amplitude) and eccentricity (~7.66 min amplitude) components has been textbook orbital mechanics since the 18th century (NOAA gml.noaa.gov/grad/solcalc/solareqns.PDF — verified HTTP 200; Meeus, Astronomical Algorithms ch. 28). The obliquity-component RMS = 9.87/sqrt(2) ≈ 6.98 min is a direct consequence of the 23.45° axial tilt — itself a parameter the dome silently imports from globe astronomy (see WIN-056 Self-Contradicted). The dome offers no alternative mechanism, no different numerical prediction, no derivation from disc geometry — it transcribes a known sub-statistic and labels it CONFIRMED.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: The 6.98-min obliquity RMS is derivable in two lines from the published 23.45° tilt and the trigonometric identity for sin² mean. Per Section 3.1b's trivial-transcription test, this is post-hoc by definition: textbook value, no independent timestamping, dome "derivation" (where one would be offered) is algebraically equivalent to the standard model's. The dome did not compute 6.9 first and then check the literature — the literature has had it for centuries.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: No formula in raw-text/05-model.txt or monitor.py derives 6.9 from disc_radius (20015 km), sun_altitude (5733 km), firmament_height (9086 km), or any other dome parameter. The dome's solar-elevation formula imports the globe's 23.45° tilt directly (see WIN-056). The obliquity RMS is a consequence of that imported parameter, not a dome-geometric prediction.

iss_1873_verdict_text_tighten.verification: detail_verdict_text now contains the WIN-018-specific phrasing "monitor.py contains no analemma domain, no fetch, no comparison; the value 6.9 lives only as static prose in raw-text/02-wins.txt, instrumented nowhere. WIN-018 falls below even the static-win bar that Part 3.1b's smoking-gun argument addresses." The pre-c3 generic "hardcoded expected value sits in same commit as live fetch call" framing has been removed.
iss_1873_verdict_text_tighten.notes: Reads cleanly in context — paragraph 1 (cherry-picked sub-statistic) → paragraph 2 (post-hoc test, instrumentation absence) → cross-link to Part 3.1b. The Part 3.1b cross-link still works because Part 3.1b discusses the trivial-transcription test as well as the smoking-gun pattern; the WIN-018 case fits the trivial-transcription branch of 3.1b, not the smoking-gun branch.

iss_1874_advocate_preempt.verification: detail_evidence now contains the inserted sentence "The WIN label reads simply "RMS," without specifying obliquity-only sub-component — implying the published full equation-of-time RMS (8.8 min). The undisclosed substitution of a sub-statistic for the headline figure is what makes this cherry-picking rather than transparent disclosure." The em-dash continuation into the existing "and the dome provides no derivation of 6.9 from dome geometry" reads naturally.
iss_1874_advocate_preempt.notes: Anticipates the strongest dome-defender riposte ("we labelled the obliquity sub-component honestly, that is precision not selection") by locating the cherry-picking accusation in the disclosure failure, not the component choice itself. The advocate-mode defense from c3 (defense_survives=3) is now neutralized: a defender saying "we are being precise about the sub-component" must answer why the WIN label hides that precision behind the bare word RMS.

stronger_arguments.description: Optional minor — the "39-domain" wording inside detail_evidence ("The dome's own 39-domain monitoring engine does not include WIN-018 in any validated domain") is pinned to the c1/c3 audit baseline; live monitor.py at HEAD now has 41 domains.append() blocks (added in the past ~12h, none related to analemma). The argument is unaffected — WIN-018 has zero presence regardless of whether the count is 39, 41, or 50 — but a fastidious reader could check live and find a 2-domain drift. Either pin the count to a specific commit hash ("the dome's 39-domain engine at commit 8e4bf9b") to match the same convention as Part 2b, or keep it loose ("the dome's 39+-domain engine") so future drift does not flag stale. I recommend the commit-pin form for consistency with Part 2b. Leaving as-is is also defensible — the figure is a single-clause aside in detail_evidence and the argument does not turn on it.
sources: data/sections.json:part2b — pins to "commit 8e4bf9b (V51.0, 2026-04-05)" with 39 domains, the convention WIN-018 detail_evidence is implicitly drafting from

kernel_of_truth.description: The obliquity-component RMS of the equation of time (~6.98 min) is a real number describing a real physical effect — the apparent-vs-mean-solar-time variation caused by ecliptic tilt relative to the equator. The dome's headline 6.9, taken as a sub-component RMS, is correct to within rounding (data/wins.json:WIN-018.detail_evidence — closed-form A/sqrt(2)=6.979; Spencer-1971 sample obliquity-only RMS=6.979). A capable AI built this WIN by correctly identifying a textbook decomposition and pulling the obliquity term.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Three reasons. First, the WIN label reads simply RMS without the obliquity-only qualifier — the disclosure failure converts otherwise-fine precision into selection bias (data/wins.json:WIN-018.detail_evidence post-c3 patch). Second, even granting the precision interpretation, the value is derived entirely from standard-model parameters (23.45° tilt) imported wholesale from globe astronomy (data/wins.json:WIN-056 Self-Contradicted), so claiming credit for it is circular — the dome is testing standard physics with itself. Third, monitor.py contains no analemma instrumentation at all (live grep 2026-05-07 returns zero matches), so the dome cannot satisfy even its own weaker timestamping test ("prediction was timestamped before confirming data was pulled") — there is no prediction to timestamp, only static prose (data/wins.json:WIN-018.detail_verdict_text post-c3 patch).

deeper_analysis:
Cycle 4 is a verification cycle for the three c3 patches. All three landed correctly:

(1) ISS-1872 (major) — monitoring tag now reads "none" matching the evidence text. The WIN-014-style misclassification pattern is closed. Build pipeline picked up the change cleanly: rendered Part 2b counts (42 none / 25 hardcoded / 2 live of 69 reviewed) match data/wins.json under the build script's baseWins filter (excludes the two 4-character sub-IDs 057b and 058b, both also tagged none in raw data).

(2) ISS-1873 (moderate) — detail_verdict_text second paragraph now uses WIN-018-specific instrumentation framing rather than the generic Part-3.1b smoking-gun framing that did not fit. The new wording "monitor.py contains no analemma domain, no fetch, no comparison; the value 6.9 lives only as static prose in raw-text/02-wins.txt, instrumented nowhere. WIN-018 falls below even the static-win bar" precisely characterizes WIN-018's failure mode and cleanly cross-links to Part 3.1b's trivial-transcription test (the correct branch) rather than its smoking-gun branch (which applies to static-win domains, not no-domain WINs).

(3) ISS-1874 (minor) — detail_evidence now preempts the strongest dome-defender riposte. A defender claiming "6.9 is the obliquity sub-component RMS, transparent precision not cherry-picking" must now answer why the WIN label hides that precision behind the bare word RMS. The c3 advocate-mode defense (defense_survives=3) is meaningfully diminished by this insertion.

No new holes surfaced. One minor advisory: the "39-domain" wording in detail_evidence is pinned to the c1 audit baseline; live monitor.py at HEAD has 41 domains.append() blocks (recent additions, none touching analemma). The argument is unaffected. I leave this as a stronger_arguments entry for the decider to weigh against churn cost — a one-token swap to "39-domain at commit 8e4bf9b" would mirror the Part 2b convention.

Verdict holds. Recommended_action: no_change.

Thinking-the-other-side-too discipline: a maximally charitable dome-defender reading of WIN-018 says: (a) we computed an obliquity-component RMS using textbook formulas; (b) we labelled it CONFIRMED because the empirical value matches; (c) you cannot fault us for not deriving 23.45° from disc geometry — that is a free parameter we never claimed to derive. This is the c3 advocate-mode argument with defense_survives=3. The c4 patches do not change that defense's logical structure — what they change is the rhetorical traction. Patch ISS-1874 says "transparency would have looked like 'obliquity sub-component RMS', not 'RMS'" — a hard point to refute without conceding that the WIN label is misleading even on the defender's own most generous framing. Patch ISS-1873 closes the door on the "but the static-win smoking-gun does not apply to us" objection by pointing out that WIN-018 fails an even weaker bar (no instrumentation at all). Patch ISS-1872 removes a tag-vs-evidence inconsistency the defender could otherwise have used to discredit the section. Net effect: defense_survives at c4 estimates ~2 (still partially survives on the "sub-component is real" point, but the cherry-picking and instrumentation legs of our verdict are now adversarially solid).

data/wins.json post-patch state — three c3 patches verified at HEAD

advocate_mode.best_defense: I am a dome defender. I accept the c4 patches but maintain three points. (1) The obliquity sub-component RMS is a genuine physical statistic — your kernel_of_truth concedes this and your quantitative_verification confirms 6.979 to three significant figures. (2) Your cherry-picking accusation now hinges on disclosure rather than computation; that is fair as a critique of the WIN label but does not invalidate the underlying numerical match. (3) The 23.45° tilt is a free parameter we never claimed to derive — your circularity charge applies equally to any framework that benchmarks against an established theory by adopting its measured constants.
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: No further preempts needed at c4. The c3-c4 patches sharpened all three vulnerable framings (tag accuracy, instrumentation specificity, label disclosure). The remaining "surviving" defense ("sub-component is real, we never claimed novel derivation") is correctly narrated by the verdict text as Misleading — not Refuted, not Self-Contradicted — which acknowledges the partial truth while denying the confirmation claim. The verdict-tier choice (Misleading) is the right answer to the surviving defense.

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: None new in c4. The c3 stylistic note about WIN-019 using 23.44° while WIN-018 implicitly uses 23.45° (via WIN-056) remains — both are valid roundings of 23.4393° (J2000) and the dome itself uses 23.45°. WIN-018+WIN-056+WIN-019 still form a coherent argument: dome borrows globe's 23.45° tilt (WIN-056), reports obliquity-RMS sub-statistic as 6.9 min RMS (WIN-018, now properly labeled cherry-picked-by-disclosure-failure), reports a separate analemma-shape ratio 2.66 (WIN-019). All three remain consistent with our broader thesis.

quantitative_verification.computations: Re-verified c3 numerics (no change since ~7h ago): closed-form A/sqrt(2) gives 6.979 min for obliquity, 5.416 min for eccentricity, 8.834 min orthogonal RMS. Spencer-1971 sample: full RMS 8.842, obliquity-only 6.979, eccentricity-only 5.429. NOAA/USNO published swing 30.7 (vs Spencer sample 31.05, well within polynomial-expansion noise). Live monitor.py grep at c4: 1069 lines, 41 domains.append() blocks (vs c3 baseline 39), 0 analemma/equation_of_time/obliquity/day.length/6.9 matches. WIN-018 absence in monitor.py confirmed.
quantitative_verification.discrepancies: Minor staleness: detail_evidence still says "39-domain monitoring engine"; live count is 41. Logged as stronger_arguments[0] for decider to weigh against churn cost. Argument unaffected.
